people.name: Gary Partis
people.summary: A&B Computing profile records a rapid path from PET BASIC to 6502 machine code, BBC Micro work, Micropower projects, Superior publication and Audiogenic contract discussion.
people.practice: Shows how an individual author could work across publishers and machines without a simple employee/company story.

people.name: Kevin Blake
people.summary: First-person portfolio connects Icon, Audiogenic, Tynesoft and later Flair/Darkblack work while repeatedly distinguishing freelance/self-employed practice from employment.
people.practice: Useful witness for contracts, conversions, bugs, returns, ports and the messy economics of 8-bit publishing.

people.name: Jason Sobell
people.summary: Appears in Kevin Blake's portfolio and forum recollections around early games and Washington club testimony.
people.practice: Treat as a documented collaborator where sources name him, not as a fully reconstructed biography.

people.name: Dave Croft
people.summary: Named by Kevin Blake as a co-author on several Tynesoft sports titles and part of the working-practice recollections.
people.practice: Represents collaborative conversion and co-author work; avoid republishing personal insults from source material.

people.name: Peter Johnson
people.summary: Named in the supplied poster and Stairway credits as a profile contributor; links to Superior/Rage require source-by-source treatment.
people.practice: Use as a research lead and relationship node, not as proof of employment without corroboration.

people.name: Peter Scott
people.summary: Supplied poster links Peter Scott to the Icon/Audiogenic/Superior network; needs profile and credit corroboration.
people.practice: Use cautiously as a network node.

people.name: Martin Edmondson
people.summary: Associated with early Reflections and later company growth; the early BBC/Electron material should be kept central.
people.practice: Shows bedroom-scale technical skill moving into durable company formation.

people.name: Nicholas Chamberlain
people.summary: Associated with early Reflections partnership in the supplied poster; needs independent contemporary corroboration.
people.practice: Use as a named research lead for Reflections origin work.

people.name: Brian Jobling
people.summary: Supplied lineage connects Brian Jobling, Tynesoft links and Zeppelin formation; current public treatment should mark this as a staff-heritage lead.
people.practice: Useful for the distinction between a person carrying knowledge forward and a company legally continuing.

people.name: Colin Courtney
people.summary: Supplied poster names Colin Courtney in the Tynesoft and Flair story; official records still required.
people.practice: Founder/director claims require Companies House or contemporary confirmation before high-confidence public phrasing.

people.name: Trevor Scott
people.summary: Supplied poster names Trevor Scott in the Tynesoft founding story; official records still required.
people.practice: Treat as a research lead until company records are added.

people.name: Phil Nixon
people.summary: Supplied poster links Phil Nixon to Rage/Venom-era activity; outside Phase 0 core.
people.practice: Keep as a later-lineage research lead.

people.name: Phil Scott
people.summary: Local research data links Phil Scott to Tynesoft and later secondary database credit leads, but the local credit graph remains incomplete.
people.practice: Show local credits and candidate external assertions separately; do not infer employment or a complete bibliography from link-only sources.

people.name: Robert Troughton
people.summary: Appears in later Pitbull/Pitbull Studio/Epic Games UK lineages; included for Teesside orbit context.
people.practice: Use in Act IV only unless stronger source expands the Teesside history.
